text: The emitter is damaged. Two of the seven copper connections are severed.
Voss's ugly solder joints are holding by faith and oxidized metal.
Tanaka kneels behind it with her back to the approach of something massive,
and she rerouts the remaining power through the intact circuits.
The output will not be a field. It will be a pulse. One pulse.
All the remaining energy in the battery discharged in a single burst at maximum amplitude.
The effect radius should reach 500 meters. Her ear drums are within that radius. She knows this.
She adjusts the output dial to maximum anyway. The foot steps behind her shake the ground.
Each one closer. Each one heavy enough to crack frozen earth.
She does not turn around. Her fingers find the activation switch. She thinks of Okafor and his
daughter's photographs. She thinks of the line behind her. She thinks of nothing at all.
Her mind goes clean and flat like a surgical field.
Craig's shadow falls over her. She sees its stretch across the snow in front of her.
She presses the switch. The pulse goes out in every direction.
A wall of sound below human hearing that hits every room on the field like a hammer on glass.
The world goes silent. Then it goes white.
